name: Nan - Indian Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm the yogurt and stir in the milk until thoroughly mixed. Remove from heat and cool.
2. In a large bowl, add the yogurt mixture to either a heavy-duty mixer or mix by hand.
3. Add baking soda, sugar, 4 tablespoons butter, eggs, and yeast. Mix until the yeast dissolves.
4. Stir in the flour and salt. Using the dough hook for your mixer, knead dough for 15-20 minutes until smooth and elastic.
5. Place dough on a plastic board and cover with a large stainless steel bowl. Let dough rise until doubled in size (about 2 hours).
6. Dust hands with flour and knead dough for a few minutes. Divide into 8 balls.
7. Roll each ball into a 10-inch pancake. Gently pull to an oval shape. Cover with plastic wrap for 20 minutes.
8. Preheat oven to 450°F (232°C) with clay tiles or use an upside-down baking sheet.
9. Mix remaining 4 tablespoons butter with poppy seeds on a small, floured plywood board.
10. Place each loaf onto the prepared board and brush the top lightly with butter/seeds.
11. Bake for about 4 minutes until bubbles form and edges turn slightly brown.
12. Remove from oven, brush bottom of loaf lightly with water, cover with plastic wrap, and serve.
